name: Important Scheduled Task Deleted/Disabled
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Important Scheduled Task Deleted/Disabled
SIGMA Description: Detects when adversaries stop services or processes by deleting or disabling their respective scheduled tasks in order to conduct data destructive activities

False positives:
- Unknown

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND (("Event ID" IN('4699', '4701')) AND (LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\systemrestore\sr%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\windows defender\%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\bitlocker%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\windowsbackup\%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\windowsupdate\%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\updateorchestrator\schedule%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\exploitguard%')) AND (NOT(("Event ID"=4699 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%$%' AND LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\windows defender\%'))) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Important Scheduled Task Deleted/Disabled on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND (("Event ID" IN('4699', '4701')) AND (LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\systemrestore\sr%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\windows defender\%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\bitlocker%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\windowsbackup\%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\windowsupdate\%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\updateorchestrator\schedule%' OR LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\exploitguard%')) AND (NOT(("Event ID"=4699 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%$%' AND LOWER("Task Name") LIKE '%\windows\windows defender\%'))) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Important Scheduled Task Deleted/Disabled

Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Event Name
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
